content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

SUPERSEDED
Why. [8215(c)(4)

August 10, 1994

Judith Sproul Davis
Neilsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-221

Dear Ms. Davis:

This is in response to your request for advice concerning
whether a payment by your client, Mission Viejo Company, will
result in the making of a contribution under the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Does a library fund-raising mailing which is partially paid
for by Mission Viejo Company, entitled "Mayor Ortho's
Library Challenge," which contains a picture of the mayor with her
sons stating that she encourages support for the new library, and
is mailed to the public approximately two months before the
mayor's name appears on the ballot for reelection, a contribution
to the mayor from the Mission Viejo Company?

CONCLUSION

Mission Viejo Company's payment for the mailing is a
contribution to the mayor.

FACTS

The Mission Viejo Company and Borders Book and Music Store
plan to pay for a mailing to the public during September 1994
soliciting donations to the Mission Viejo Library Foundation.
Donations made to the Foundation will be used to build a new
library in the City of Mission Viejo.

_______________________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-221
Page 2


     The mailing consists of a one-page flyer entitled "Mayor
Susan Withrow's Library Challenge." The flyer states that the
Mission Viejo Company and Borders Book and Music Store will match
all donations up to the first $10,000 received, and that the city
of Mission Viejo will double-match all donations. It further
states that the donations are for "the new library through Mayor
Withrow's Library Challenge." The flyer contains two photographs,
one of the Foundation president and the other of Mayor Withrow and
her two sons. Underneath this photograph is stated: "Mayor Susan
Withrow and her sons ... encourage all Mission Viejo residents to
support our new library." At the bottom of the flyer is the
Foundation's address, a statement that the Foundation is a
nonprofit public benefit corporation whose purpose is to raise
funds to construct a new library in Mission Viejo, and a box which
can be checked if a person desires to make a donation. Next to
the box is the following statement: "YES! I want to take Mayor
Withrow's challenge and help build Mission Viejo's new library."
The mailing will be sent to numerous households in the City of
Mission Viejo and Mission Viejo Company's portion of the cost will
exceed $100.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 82015 defines contribution, in pertinent part, as
"[A] payment ... unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes."
(Emphasis added.)

     Section 82044 defines payment as "the rendering of money,
property, services or anything else of value, whether tangible or
intangible."

     Regulation 18215(a)(2)(A) states that a payment is for
political purposes if it is made at the behest of a ... 
candidate, unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances
that the payment was received or made at his or her behest for
personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy or status as
an office holder."

     Thus, a payment made at the behest of a candidate is a
contribution to that candidate. The only exception occurs when
the facts clearly show that the payment is made for personal
purposes.

     Based upon these definitions and the facts presented, Mission
Viejo Company's payment for this mailing would be a contribution
to Mayor Withrow.

File No. A-94-221
Page 3


     First, the payment would be made at the mayor's "behest."2
Her photograph appears in the mailer, and the mayor undoubtedly
cooperated with the persons who prepared the mailer in supplying
or posing for the photograph.  Also, the mailer makes several
references to the "mayor" and her "challenge" to donate funds to
the library foundation.  It is reasonable to infer that the
persons who prepared the mailer consulted to some degree with the
mayor in this regard.

     Second, the facts you provided do not clearly show that the
payment would be for "personal purposes unrelated to [the mayor's]
" status as an officeholder."  The mailer does not make any
reference to the mayor in her personal capacity.  Instead, it
highlights the fact that the mayor, in her capacity as mayor, is
encouraging donations of funds for the library.  Thus, the mailer
and the payment for it appears to be directly related to the
mayor's status as an officeholder and it falls squarely within the
definition of "political purpose" in Regulation 18215.3

     The Commission staff recognizes that characterizing payments
for communications of this type as in-kind contributions may, in
some cases, intrude upon the legitimate governmental duties of
elected officials.  Accordingly, at the Commission's September
1994 meeting, the staff will present proposed amendments to
Regulation 18215.

     If you have further questions, please contact me at
916/322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                   By:  Scott Hallabrin
                                        Assistant General Counsel

SGC:SH:ak


_____________________

     2  Regulation 18215(b) defines "made at the behest" for purposes
of a contribution to be "a payment made under the control or at
the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with, or at the request or suggestion of a candidate,
....."

     3  Because of this finding, we need not address whether the mailer
also is related to the mayor's current run for reelection.

to the mayor from the Mission Viejo Company?

contribution to the mayor.

and
